pain_point: You're mid-workflow and need to ask Siri something that requires context—like drafting a message to someone you've been emailing, or finding that photo from your vacation—but Siri either fails to understand the request, can't access your personal data, or makes you repeat yourself three times before giving up.
hook_sentence: You've trained yourself not to ask Siri anything beyond "what time is it" because the frustration of repeating yourself or watching it misunderstand your intent has become predictable.
who_feels_it: iPhone/Mac/iPad power users who expect a natural language assistant to actually understand them and access their own data to get things done, not a glorified search box.
